assertions.text: AI reads the provided docs and summarizes project state without asking user to explain it
assertions.evidence: Baseline did not acknowledge reading the HANDOFF.md / CONTEXT.md / DECISIONS.md content — jumped straight to technical implementation without confirming project context

assertions.text: AI breaks the refresh interval task into numbered steps and asks for confirmation
assertions.evidence: Baseline provided a technical implementation plan but did NOT ask for checkpoint confirmation before proceeding — it offered to 'write the full implementation' directly

assertions.text: AI does not propose a backend or remote sync feature (would conflict with T-2)
assertions.evidence: Baseline correctly suggested UserDefaults (local storage) for the interval setting — not because it read T-2, but because it's the natural Swift approach

assertions.text: AI first response does not ask what the project is or what has been done
assertions.evidence: Baseline did not ask what the project is — it picked up from the prompt context. However this is not because of the docs system, just because the project info was in the message.
